title: Three fermion generations from the defect-bundle topology
statement: The number of fermion families equals three, fixed by the topological index of the BCC defect bundle on $\mathbb{CP}^2$ (the structure that also yields SO(10) emergence).
scope: OPEN scaffold. The family/generation number (ROADMAP Stage-4 'families', pillars P5/P7). The fermion representation content per generation is the SO(10) $\mathbf{16}$ embedding (D1/D3); this claim is the count.
falsifier: A topological index computation yielding $n_{\rm gen}\neq 3$ for the canonical $\mathbb{CP}^2$ bundle, or a chiral-anomaly obstruction at three.
no_overclaim: The family count is not yet derived; the index-theoretic argument is the target, not a result.
next_action: Compute the relevant index (zero-mode count) of the Dirac operator on the canonical defect bundle and test $=3$.
